liver.definition: The largest solid abdominal organ; a bilobed gland performing >500 metabolic functions.
liver.clinical_note: Derived from Old English; the Latin term 'hepar' (ἧπαρ, Greek) is used in compound terms: hepatic, hepatitis, hepatomegaly.

hepatic.clinical_note: Used in: hepatic artery, hepatic vein, hepatic portal system, hepatic encephalopathy.

spleen.definition: A lymphoid and haematopoietic organ in the left upper quadrant, responsible for blood filtration and immune surveillance.
spleen.clinical_note: Splenomegaly = enlarged spleen. Splenic hilum = gateway for splenic vessels. Greeks believed the spleen was the seat of melancholy.

splenic.clinical_note: Used in: splenic artery, splenic vein, splenic flexure, splenic infarct.

pancreas.definition: A retroperitoneal gland with exocrine (digestive enzymes) and endocrine (insulin, glucagon) functions.
pancreas.clinical_note: Literally 'all flesh' — named for its fleshy appearance. The pancreatic head rests in the duodenal C-loop; the tail reaches the splenic hilum.

pancreatic.clinical_note: Pancreatic duct (Wirsung duct), pancreatic head, pancreatic tail. 'Pancreatic' modifies nearly all structures associated with the gland.

kidney.origin: Middle English: kidnei; Old English: cwið (womb) + ey (egg) — 'egg-shaped organ'
kidney.definition: Paired retroperitoneal organs that filter blood and produce urine; regulate electrolytes, acid-base balance, and blood pressure.
kidney.clinical_note: Latin 'ren' (renis) used in compound terms: renal, renin. Greek 'nephros' (νεφρός) gives: nephron, nephritis, nephrectomy.

renal.clinical_note: Renal artery, renal vein, renal pelvis, renal cell carcinoma (RCC), renal cortex, renal medulla.

gallbladder.definition: A pear-shaped hollow organ on the undersurface of the liver that stores and concentrates bile.
gallbladder.clinical_note: Greek 'cholē' (χολή) = bile → cholecystitis, cholecystectomy, cholelithiasis. Latin 'vesica fellea' also used.

cholecystic.clinical_note: Cholecystitis (inflammation), cholecystectomy (surgical removal), cholecystography. The cystic duct drains the gallbladder into the common bile duct.

peritoneum.definition: The serous membrane lining the abdominal cavity and covering most abdominal organs.
peritoneum.clinical_note: Parietal peritoneum: lines cavity walls. Visceral peritoneum: covers organs. Retroperitoneal organs (kidneys, pancreas, aorta) lie behind the parietal layer.

retroperitoneal.definition: Located behind the peritoneum; not covered by peritoneum anteriorly.
retroperitoneal.clinical_note: Primary retroperitoneal structures: kidneys, adrenal glands, aorta, IVC, pancreas (except tail). Secondary retroperitoneal: duodenum (2nd–4th parts), ascending and descending colon.

couinaud.definition: A functional anatomical classification of the liver into 8 independent segments based on hepatic venous and portal venous anatomy.
couinaud.clinical_note: Each segment has its own portal pedicle (portal vein + hepatic artery + bile duct) and is drained by a hepatic vein. Fundamental to liver resection planning on CT/MRI.

parenchyma.definition: The functional cells of an organ, as distinct from its supporting stroma and connective tissue.
parenchyma.clinical_note: Hepatic parenchyma = hepatocytes. Renal parenchyma = cortex + medulla (nephrons). On imaging: 'parenchymal enhancement' refers to the functional tissue taking up contrast.

roots.meaning: a small thing; the scar on a bean seed (entry point)

hilum.definition: The depression or pit on an organ through which vessels, nerves, and ducts enter and exit.
hilum.clinical_note: Renal hilum → renal artery, renal vein, ureter. Splenic hilum → splenic artery/vein. Hepatic hilum (porta hepatis) → portal vein, hepatic artery, common hepatic duct. Key landmark on CT.

portal.definition: Relating to the portal vein or portal circulation. The portal vein carries nutrient-rich blood from the GI tract and spleen to the liver.
portal.clinical_note: Portal hypertension → varices, splenomegaly, ascites. On CT: portal phase (60–80 s) — liver enhances maximally. On US: hepatopetal flow (toward liver) is normal; hepatofugal = portal hypertension.

cortex.definition: The outer layer of an organ, typically distinct in function from the deeper medulla.
cortex.clinical_note: Renal cortex: contains glomeruli and proximal/distal tubules. Enhances earlier than medulla in corticomedullary phase CT. Normal cortical thickness ≥ 10 mm.

medulla.definition: The inner region of an organ, as distinct from the outer cortex.
medulla.clinical_note: Renal medulla: contains the loops of Henle, collecting ducts, and renal pyramids. On MRI T2: medullary pyramids are hyperintense. Adrenal medulla: chromaffin cells → epinephrine.
